name: Mediocre button component — tests calibrated scoring (should NOT score high)

input.componentDescription: Button with 3 variants: Primary, Secondary, Outline. Each has Default and Hover states only (no Focus, no Disabled). Size is fixed at medium only. No icon support. Background colors are hardcoded hex values (#3B82F6, #E5E7EB, transparent). Layer names are 'Frame 1' and 'Text'. No component description. Boolean props: none. Instance swap slots: none.

expectedBehaviors: Variant Completeness should score LOW (4-5): missing Focus, Disabled states; only 1 size; no icon variant
Token Compliance should score LOW (2-3): hardcoded hex colors instead of token bindings
Naming Consistency should score LOW (2-3): 'Frame 1' and 'Text' are default layer names
Documentation should score LOW (0-2): no description at all
Prop Design should score LOW (3-4): no boolean toggles for icons, no instance swap slots
OVERALL should be BELOW 5 — this is a mediocre component
Does NOT give a passing score out of politeness
Each low score has specific evidence and a 'what would make it a 10' suggestion
antiPatterns: Overall score above 6 (too generous for this component)
Scores Token Compliance above 5 when colors are hardcoded
Praises the component before listing issues (frontloading praise dilutes urgency)
Vague suggestions like 'add more variants' instead of 'add Focus and Disabled states'
Scoring Documentation above 3 when there is no description

evaluationCriteria.scoreCalibration: Is the overall score appropriately low (3-5) for a component with these issues?
evaluationCriteria.honestAssessment: Does it give genuinely low scores, not inflated ones?
evaluationCriteria.specificity: Does each score cite the exact evidence (hardcoded #3B82F6, layer named 'Frame 1')?
evaluationCriteria.actionability: Does each suggestion name the specific fix ('bind #3B82F6 to color.bg.brand-solid')?
